settings.headerText: BARCLAYS INTERNAL AUDIT (BIA) | FORMAL REPORT TITLE

mainContent.text: This sentence must be a separated paragraph to any other narrative underneath and succinctly explain the driver of the opinion – this sentence will be used for BAC reporting summaries: The <insert rating> rating over <insert in scope area> is driven by <provide summary>.

For Post-Implementation Change Audits, clearly articulate if the rating is driven as a result of 1) programme / project governance (i.e. the Programme is the adverse rating driver), or 2) what has been delivered by the programme / project.

This next section can be used to further expand on the drivers for the rating, allowing the executive reader to understand the basis of BIA’s opinion and why the rating is appropriate and not more or less adverse. Ensure that the ‘so what’ is answered and clear – this should be insightful and go beyond the surface level observations or a list of facts.

Additional points for consideration:
•	Inform the AE about essential details and actions required, prioritising key information.
•	Stick to the facts and avoid jargon, acronyms and technical language

•	Conclude if Management needs to improve its effectiveness at self-identification of issues (with rationale) or conclude if they are effective at self-identification of issues (with rationale).

•	Conclude if Management needs to improve its effectiveness at reducing risk in their processes through simplification and/or automation of controls (with rationale) or conclude if they are effective (with rationale). If automated controls were not tested by BIA, explain why.

•	Include narrative on Management’s overall approach to risk and control.

•	If the report is adverse, to add value and insight, BIA should comment on the quality of the controls in the Control Library.

sections.text: Additional Context
Business Context
Provide a brief description of the area reviewed, the business objectives, key stakeholders and regulatory environment where applicable.
Audit Scope
Provide a brief description of the audit scope and list the risk statements/hypothesis. Where data analytics was used as part of testing, this must be stated.
SMF and Legal Entity Impact

1. Include commentary in the below table on the impact of the audit opinion (CE) and issues on the BBPLC, BBUK, BBI, IHC, BBDE Legal Entities. Factors to include but limited to: • Differences in the operation or frequency of the control to the legal entity • Impact of CE or audit issues that LE Service Recipient should be aware of • Impact of control homogeneity where applicable • Regulatory requirements or regulatory requests to provide coverage 2. Also include commentary where the relevance of the audit opinion differs across legal entities. If the driver of an adverse assessment related to findings in one legal entity, but not another, specifically state this. For example:
  • The critical issues raised in this audit have similar significance to both BBPLC and BBUKPLC.
  • The issues in this audit predominantly impact BBPLC, due to the mitigating controls in place in BBUKPLC.
  • The issues in this audit impact the availability of systems used for retail customer processes, so have greater impact on BBUKPLC than BBPLC.
  • BBPLC represents 80% of risk limits covered in this audit, BBUKPLC represents 20%

rows.text: Insert a few lines of commentary (here and below) per above guidance if applicable.

sections.text: BIA has assessed this audit report is most relevant to the following SMF or SMF equivalent role holders:
In addition to the SMF or SMF equivalents listed above, BIA has assessed this audit report is most relevant to the following Legal Entity Executive Committee (LE ExCo) Members:

The SMF or SMF equivalents and LE ExCo members named in this report represent BIA’s assessment of the individuals for whom the report has the most relevance and does not introduce any new responsibilities or place any additional expectations on these individuals. It will not necessarily be an exhaustive list of individuals that may have an interest in the report as a result of their responsibilities. It is meant to supplement existing arrangement and support individuals by providing further transparency on the impact of the audit relevant to their individual areas of accountability.

Guidance:
All included names should be agreed with the LE CIA and listed alphabetically in the tables.
The above Legal Entity Stakeholder table is applicable for Barclays US LLC and Barclays Bank Delaware stakeholder names only. Where the table is not used, delete and remove references to Legal Entity Stakeholders in the narratives.

Summary of issues identified by BIA
Summary of known issues identified

Delete if report was not translated:
The original [opinion/report] [and the underlying documentation/materials] [was/were] prepared in the English language and this translation is provided for your convenience only. If there is any conflict between the English version and this translation, the English version shall prevail.

This translation was prepared by [NAME OF TRANSLATION SERVICES], an independent translation service. No representation or warranty, either express or implied, is made as to, and no reliance should be placed on, the accuracy, completeness, correctness or reliability of this translation.

If the audit was not executed in line with BIA Methodology because of a specific internal auditing requirement of an authoritative body, country specific/regional law or regulation, this must be disclosed within the final audit report. Please contact BIA Professional Practices in this scenario.

Disclaimer: The information and opinions contained in this report are provided as of the date of this report. This report is confidential and prepared solely for the benefit of Barclays and may not be relied upon by any other person other than distribution to Barclays’ recipients, Barclays’ regulators or its external auditors. This report should not be distributed to any other person, without the express written consent of the Barclays Group Chief Internal Auditor or one of their Direct Reports. Barclays Internal Audit are under no obligation to update any information or opinions contained in this report.
Appendix 1 – Issue and Report Assessments
Audit issues are assessed as:

rows.text: The issue meets the Severity and Probability criteria for a Critical issue in the Barclays Risk & Issue Classification Matrix (RICM). The issue should be escalated to Barclays Plc management.
The issue meets the Severity and Probability criteria for a Major issue in the Barclays Risk & Issue Classification Matrix (RICM). The issue should be escalated to Legal Entity management.
The issue meets the Severity and Probability criteria for a Limited issue in the Barclays Risk & Issue Classification Matrix (RICM). The issue should be escalated to local management.

sections.text:
Rated audit reports are assessed as:
• Control environment

rows.text: Effective and sustainable controls, automated where appropriate, using strategic processes and infrastructure. Barclays are managing long-term residual risk.
Overall effective and sustainable controls. There may be some control weaknesses, and controls may not be automated or using strategic processes to manage long-term residual risk.
Partially effective controls, in design or execution, or effective controls are not yet fully sustainable. Barclays have not fully mitigated residual risk.
Ineffective control design or execution, or missing controls, such that there is significant unmitigated residual risk impacting Barclays Strategic Legal Entities.
